steps.title: Use the two demand points
steps.content: Treat price as $x$ and demand as $y$. The two given points are $(40,20000)$ and $(60,15000)$.

steps.title: Find the linear rate of change
steps.content: The slope is $$\frac{15000-20000}{60-40}=\frac{-5000}{20}=-250.$$ Demand decreases by $250$ units for every $1$ dollar increase in price.

steps.title: Evaluate at the target price
steps.content: From $P=40$ to $P=55$ is a $15$ dollar increase, so demand changes by $$15(-250)=-3750.$$ Starting from $20000$ units gives $$20000-3750=16250.$$

steps.title: Check the choice
steps.content: The model $y=-250x+30000$ gives $y=16250$ when $x=55$. That matches choice A.
